name: Loop of Tarbes
region_intro: Jogging around Tarbes offers a blend of urban green spaces and natural landscapes at the foot of the Pyrenees massif. The region is characterized by flat river paths along the Adour, serene lake loops, and expansive city parks. These diverse settings provide varied terrain for running, from paved surfaces within the city to natural footpaths around the lakes. The proximity to the Pyrenees also offers inspiring views, even from lower-elevation routes. Best jogging routes around Tarbes The most popular jogging route is Loop of Tarbes, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail that takes about 55 minutes to complete. This moderate route primarily follows paved surfaces within the city. Another top favourite among local runners is Lake Soues loop from Tarbes, a moderate 8.3 miles (13.3 km) path. This route offers scenic views around Lac de Soues, often connecting to the Caminadour along the Adour river. Local runners also love the Adour River loop from Tarbes, a 7.6 miles (12.3 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 16 minutes. Jogging around Tarbes is defined by urban green spaces like the Massey Garden, tranquil lake paths, and riverside trails along the Adour. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy city loops to more challenging routes with slight elevation gains. The routes in Tarbes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 130 reviews. More than 9,500 runners have used komoot to explore Tarbes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Tarbes?
A: There are over 700 running routes around Tarbes, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. This includes 59 easy routes, over 500 moderate options, and 143 more challenging trails for experienced runners.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging routes in Tarbes?
A: Jogging routes in Tarbes offer a diverse mix of terrain. You'll find flat river paths along the Adour, serene loops around lakes like Soues and Bours, and expansive urban green spaces such as the Massey Garden and Haras de Tarbes. Surfaces range from paved paths within the city to natural footpaths around the lakes, with inspiring views of the Pyrenees often visible.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Tarbes offers several easy running routes perfect for beginners or family outings. Many of the urban green spaces, like the &lt;strong&gt;Massey Garden&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Haras de Tarbes&lt;/strong&gt;, provide gentle, scenic paths. For a slightly longer but still moderate option, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e880643074/loop-of-tarbes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop of Tarbes&lt;/a&gt; is mostly paved and suitable for all levels.

Q: Where can I find routes that feature lakes or rivers?
A: Tarbes is rich in routes featuring water. The &lt;strong&gt;Lakes of Soues and Bours&lt;/strong&gt; are popular for peaceful nature runs. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39173509" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Soues loop from Tarbes&lt;/a&gt;, which often connects to the scenic &lt;strong&gt;Caminadour&lt;/strong&gt; along the Adour river. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41435056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Adour River loop from Tarbes&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through beautiful riverine landscapes.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging trails in Tarbes?
A: The running trails in Tarbes are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 130 reviews. More than 9,500 runners have explored the area using komoot, often praising the varied terrain, from tranquil lake paths to convenient city loops, and the inspiring views of the Pyrenees.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Yes, for those seeking a greater challenge, Tarbes and its surroundings offer more difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10530431" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Adour River – Lac de Bours loop from Tarbes&lt;/a&gt; is a longer, difficult route spanning nearly 18 km. Further afield, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3993372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac du Gabas loop from Luquet&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24679267" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arrêt-Darré Lake loop from Lespouey&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gains, ideal for experienced trail runners.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails around Tarbes?
A: Many of the outdoor paths around Tarbes, especially those in natural areas like the lake loops and riverside trails, are suitable for running with dogs. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash and be mindful of local regulations, especially in urban parks or protected areas.

Q: Are there any hidden gems or unique places to explore while running in Tarbes?
A: Absolutely! The &lt;strong&gt;Massey Garden&lt;/strong&gt; is an 11-hectare 19th-century garden in the heart of Tarbes, offering a beautiful and tranquil setting for a run. Another unique spot is the &lt;strong&gt;Haras de Tarbes&lt;/strong&gt;, a national stud farm that doubles as an 8-hectare natural park with sumptuous gardens, perfect for a picturesque jog.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I might see along the running routes?
A: While running around Tarbes, you can encounter several interesting sights. The routes often pass by the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5606547" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arrêt-Darré Lake&lt;/a&gt;. Within the city, you'll run through vibrant areas of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5898026" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarbes&lt;/a&gt; itself, including its beautiful parks. For more information on nearby attractions, you can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/1525759/attractions-around-tarbes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Attractions around Tarbes guide&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any running routes accessible by public transport in Tarbes?
A: Many routes that start directly in Tarbes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10529222" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Echez Park – University hub loop from Tarbes&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38526284" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarbes – Tarbes loop from Tarbes&lt;/a&gt;, are easily accessible via the city's public transport network. For routes slightly outside the city center, local bus services can often get you close to trailheads. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for specific starting points.

Q: When is the best time of year to go jogging in Tarbes?
A: Tarbes offers good jogging conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late evening runs are often preferred. Even in winter, many routes remain accessible, especially the urban and lower-elevation lake paths, offering crisp air and unique views.
tips: A Pyrenean city with an industrial tradition at the foot of the Pyrenees massif, whose peaks (starting with the Pic du Midi de Bigorre) stand out clearly in the urban landscape!
A space near Tarbes dedicated to sport and culture. Lots of organized events but you can just bathe in them
